sonnet_review.reason: Sensational wording that frames the dispute in emotive terms rather than neutrally describing it.
Evaluative descriptor that signals judgment about rarity without attribution; arguably subjective even if the situation is rare.

sonnet_review.biasedText: Powell's rebuke of Trump was in some ways even more shocking
sonnet_review.reason: “Even more shocking” is a value judgment presented as narrative fact.
Characterizes Powell’s tone and intent with evaluative language rather than neutral description.
Signals the author’s evaluation of significance without sourcing.
Infers Powell’s internal state/motivation; interpretive subhead rather than neutral labeling.
Predictive and emotive phrasing that goes beyond cautious analysis.

sonnet_review.biasedText: Economists, academics and former Fed officials broadly agree that safeguarding Fed independence is necessary for the soundness of the US economy.
sonnet_review.reason: Appeal to authority and broad consensus without acknowledging dissent; leans on elite voices to frame correctness.

gpt_review.biasedText: The gloves are off in the heated clash between Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and President Donald Trump.
gpt_review.reason: Dramatic idiom and emotive adjective amplify a conflict narrative.

gpt_review.biasedText: Powell’s rebuke of Trump was in some ways even more shocking.
gpt_review.reason: “Even more shocking” is a subjective intensifier presented as narrative.
Evaluative framing of Powell’s response that implies heroism/valor.

gpt_review.biasedText: It enables the Fed to do its core mission: making jobs plentiful and keeping prices in check, even when politically imprudent.
gpt_review.reason: Presents positive outcomes as inherent to independence in the author’s voice without sourcing; value-laden phrasing (“making jobs plentiful”).

gpt_review.biasedText: Economists, academics and former Fed officials broadly agree that safeguarding Fed independence is necessary for the soundness of the US economy.
gpt_review.reason: One-sided appeal to consensus among select elites; no acknowledgment of counterarguments.

gpt_review.biasedText: Trump has asked for more. Trump has said the United States should have rates that are among the lowest in the world and that he should have a say in rate decisions.
gpt_review.reason: The phrasing is largely neutral reportage of Trump’s stated positions; “asked for more” and “should have a say” are not inherently emotive or pejorative here.

gpt_review.biasedText: The Fed has been forthcoming about the renovation’s cost overruns and its collaboration with other agencies, even posting an FAQ on its website with all those details. The Fed last summer also provided a guided tour of the renovation to a handful of reporters.
gpt_review.reason: Authorial assertion favoring the Fed’s transparency without balancing skepticism or opposing evidence.
